tex:
By writing each number correct to 1 significant figure, find an estimate for the value of
\[
\dfrac{2.8 \times 82.6}{27.8 - 13.9}
\]

The number of bowls of hot soup sold decreases when the temperature rises.

What type of correlation does this statement describe?

Joseph spends $\dfrac{5}{24}$ of one week’s earnings to buy a jacket.
The cost of the jacket is \$56.50.

Calculate the amount Joseph earns in a week.

Without using a calculator, work out $2 \dfrac{1}{4} \times 3 \dfrac{2}{3}$.
You must show all your working and give your answer as a mixed number in its simplest form.
Write $0.3\dot{7}$ as a fraction.

Calculate $4.8 \times 10^{6} + 3.7 \times 10^{7}$.
Give your answer in standard form.
Find the highest common factor (HCF) of 36 and 84.

The population of one variety of butterfly is decreasing exponentially at a rate of 34\% per year.
At the end of 2014, the population was 125.9 million.

Calculate the population at the end of 2019.

(a) These are the first four terms of a sequence.

$29 \quad 22 \quad 15 \quad 8$

Write down the next two terms.

 , 

(b) These are the first five terms of another sequence.

$4 \quad 7 \quad 12 \quad 19 \quad 28$

Find the $n$th term.

Points $E$, $F$, $G$ and $H$ lie on the circle and $EG = EH$.
$HF$ and $EG$ intersect at $K$.
$ET$ is a tangent to the circle at $E$.
Angle $FET = 47^\circ$ and angle $FEG = 25^\circ$.

Find the value of $x$.

Some students were asked how many books they each had in their school bags.
The table shows some of this information.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Number of books & 5 & 6 & 7 & 8 & 9 & 10 \\
\hline
Frequency & 4 & 5 & $x$ & 11 & 7 & 5 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
The mean number of books is 7.6.

Calculate the value of $x$.
texHtml:
Some students were asked how many books they each had in their school bags. The table shows some of this information.
Number of books5678910
Frequency45$x$1175
The mean number of books is 7.6. Calculate the value of $x$.

tex:
Solve the simultaneous equations.
You must show all your working.
\[
\begin{aligned}
x - y &= 7 \\
x^2 + y &= 149
\end{aligned}
\]

(a) A bag of rice has a mass of 25 kg, correct to the nearest kilogram.

Calculate the lower bound of the total mass of 10 of these bags.

(b) Virat has 200 metres of wire, correct to the nearest metre.
He cuts the wire into $n$ pieces of length 3 metres, correct to the nearest 20 centimetres.

Calculate the largest possible value of $n$.

The diagram shows a pyramid $VABCD$ with a rectangular base.
$V$ is vertically above $M$, the intersection of the diagonals $AC$ and $BD$.
$AB = 12$ cm, $BC = 10$ cm and $VC = 14$ cm.

Calculate the angle that $VC$ makes with the base $ABCD$.

A curve has equation $y = x^3 - 2x^2 + 5$.

Find the coordinates of its two stationary points.
